description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular form and a deep, dark red wooden or wood-like casing. The front features a convex glass screen surrounded by a thick, black bezel, with a metallic speaker grille and two large rotary knobs below, likely for channel and volume control. The sides are smooth and unadorned, showcasing the rich red finish, while the back panel is dark brown with ventilation holes, multiple control dials, and a protruding cylindrical component, possibly the cathode ray tube. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century televisions, with a sturdy build and retro aesthetic. The television sits on small feet, elevating it slightly above the surface. No visible brand markings or modern ports are present, emphasizing its classic, analog nature.
description: A vintage, box-shaped television with a red wooden casing, convex glass screen, and prominent rotary knobs on the front. The back features ventilation holes and a protruding tube, evoking a classic mid-century design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the convex glass screen, thick black bezel, metallic speaker grille, and two large rotary knobs.
description_view_1: Left side view displays the smooth, deep red casing with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a dark brown panel with ventilation holes, multiple dials, and a protruding cylindrical tube.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the uninterrupted red casing and the side of the protruding tube.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black bezel, matte finish, rectangular with rounded corners, no visible branding or ornamentation.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large rotary knobs and possible buttons on the lower front panel, arranged in a row.
